You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).
To continue using code reviews, you can upgrade your account or add credits to your account and enable them for code reviews in your [settings](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/code-review).

DeepSource Code Review

We reviewed changes in cba47e2...05ad6fe on this pull request. Below is the summary for the review, and you can see the individual issues we found as inline review comments.

See full review on DeepSource Ôåù

> [!IMPORTANT] > Some issues found as part of this review are outside of the diff in this pull request and aren't shown in the inline review comments due to GitHub's API limitations. You can see those issues on the DeepSource dashboard.

PR Report Card

Overall Grade  

Focus Area: Hygiene 		Security  

Reliability  

Complexity  

Hygiene  

Feedback

  • Avoiding idiomatic C# abstractions
    • Repetitive verbose null checks and an abstract-only class hint at reluctance to use interfaces, pattern matching, and null-coalescing. Embrace language idioms (interfaces, ?. / ??, pattern matching) to remove boilerplate and clarify intent.
  • Large methods hoarding responsibilities
    • High cyclomatic functions lead to split conditionals, duplicated dictionary lookups, and tangled logic. Break big routines into focused helpers, consolidate related conditions, and cache TryGetValue results to reduce branching and accidental repetition.
  • Implicit assumptions about time and state
    • Use of local time and inconsistent defensive access shows code assumes runtime context and present keys. Centralize a UTC time provider, validate/normalize inputs at boundaries, and use TryGetValue to avoid fragile, environment-dependent behavior.

Code Review Summary

Analyzer Status Updated (UTC) Details
C# Mar 10, 2026 12:48a.m. Review Ôåù
Secrets Mar 10, 2026 12:48a.m. Review Ôåù

­ƒôØ Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds a large, modular NinjaTrader strategy V12_002 implementing multi-account SIMA orchestration: six entry modes (FFMA, OR, RMA, MOMO, TREND, RETEST), IPC server, actor-style serialized command executor (Enqueue), comprehensive order lifecycle/follower sync, reaper/compliance subsystems, ATR sizing, trailing, and UI callbacks. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Configuration & Small Types**
`V12_002.Properties.cs`, `V12_002.Constants.cs`, `V12_002.AccountUpdate.cs`, `V12_002.Atm.cs`, `V12_002.Data.cs`|Adds extensive strategy properties/enums, constants (StrategyName/Version), and small placeholder types (AccountUpdate, Atm, Data).| |**Entry Engines**
`V12_002.Entries.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.FFMA.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.OR.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.RMA.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.MOMO.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs`, `V12_002.Entries.Retest.cs`, `src/V12_002.Entries.*`|Introduces modular entry implementations for FFMA/OR/RMA/MOMO/TREND/RETEST: validation, ATR-based stops, multi-target ladders, sizing, activation/deactivation, manual-entry variants, and many private helper methods.| |**Order Callbacks & Management**
`V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, `V12_002.Orders.Management.cs`, `src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.cs`|Implements comprehensive order lifecycle handlers, target-fill application, bracket submit/refresh, stop/target replacement FSMs, follower-master synchronization, reconciliation, and flattening logic.| |**Core Strategy & Actor Model**
`V12_002.cs`, `src/V12_002.cs`, `V12_002.Symmetry.cs`, `src/V12_002.Symmetry.cs`|Adds public V12_002 Strategy foundation (BUILD_TAG, types, lifecycle overrides), OR utilities, and an inline actor/Enqueue drain replacing many global locks for serialized state mutations.| |**Reaper & Risk Audit**
`V12_002.REAPER.cs`, `V12_002.LogicAudit.cs`, `src/V12_002.LogicAudit.cs`|Adds Reaper safety/audit system (flatten/repair/naked-stop queues, background loop) and a risk-logic audit routine producing multi-case verification outputs.| |**Compliance, IPC & UI Modules**
`V12_002.UI.Compliance.cs`, `V12_002.UI.IPC.cs`, `src/V12_002.UI.IPC.cs`, `V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`, `src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`, `V12_002.UI.Sizing.cs`, `src/V12_002.UI.Sizing.cs`|Adds Apex Compliance Hub, multi-client TCP IPC server and command handlers, UI hotkeys/chart-click handlers, ATR sizing and SyncPendingOrders, and enqueued UI-trigger wiring.| |**Trailing & Position Management**
`V12_002.Trailing.cs`, `src/V12_002.Trailing.cs`|Implements adaptive trailing-stop system (BE/trails T1ÔÇôT3), MoveSpecificTarget, stop-replacement cleanup, and follower-aware FSMs; many lockÔåÆactor adjustments.| |**Top-level Engine**
`V12_002.cs` (root) |Introduces central generated engine class with lifecycle hooks (OnStateChange, OnBarUpdate, OnMarketData), IPC scaffolding, multi-account dictionaries, and orchestration wiring.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client as IPC Client participant IPC as IPC Server participant Strategy as V12_002 Actor participant Broker as Broker/API Client->>IPC: SEND_COMMAND (e.g., EXECUTE_ENTRY) IPC->>Strategy: Enqueue(command) rgba(0,128,255,0.5) Strategy->>Strategy: Validate & calculate (size/stop/targets) rgba(0,200,100,0.5) Strategy->>Broker: SubmitOrder/CancelOrder (entry/stop/targets) rgba(255,128,0,0.5) Broker-->>Strategy: OrderUpdate / Execution rgba(255,0,0,0.5) Strategy->>IPC: Broadcast status update (optional) rgba(0,128,255,0.5) ``` ## Estimated code review effort ­ƒÄ» 5 (Critical) | ÔÅ▒´©Å ~120 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - mkalhitti-cloud/universal-or-strategy#29 ÔÇö Overlapping edits to RETEST entry implementations and synchronization (ExecuteRetestEntry/ExecuteRetestManualEntry) that may conflict with enqueue/lock changes. - mkalhitti-cloud/universal-or-strategy#32 ÔÇö Related changes to FFMA entry paths and synchronization/rollback behavior (ExecuteFFMAEntry et al.) that overlap with this PR. ## Suggested labels `SIMA / Fleet`, `REAPER` ## Poem > ­ƒÉç I hop through code with nimble paws, > Enqueue each beat and mind the laws. > Targets five and reaper's song, > Fleet-wide harmony hums along. > V12 bloomsÔÇölet trading paws applause!

The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |

### ­ƒñû Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI)

Greetings. I am the Gemini CLI, the Antigravity Auto-Auditor. I have analyzed this pull request against the `GEMINI.md` project standards.

The proposed changes constitute a major architectural refactoring, replacing the established `lock`-based concurrency model with a queue-based actor model and partitioning the primary strategy file into numerous partial classes.

This review has identified multiple critical violations of the `GEMINI.md` standards. The pull request cannot be approved.

### **Audit Verdict: FAILED**

---

### **Critical Violations**

#### 1. **Concurrency Standard Violation (CRITICAL)**

The PR completely removes the `stateLock` object and all `lock(stateLock)` blocks, replacing them with a queue-based `Enqueue` actor model.

*   **Standard:** `GEMINI.md` -> **Concurrency**: "All state mutations (activePositions, expectedPositions) MUST be guarded by `lock(stateLock)`."
*   **Violation:** This is a direct and fundamental violation of the mandated concurrency model. While the actor model is a valid pattern, it is not the one specified by our standards. An architectural change of this magnitude requires a formal update to `GEMINI.md` before implementation. All state mutations are now unguarded by the required `lock`.
*   **File(s) Affected:** `V12_002.cs`, `V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, `V12_002.Trailing.cs`, `V12_002.LogicAudit.cs`, and others. The change is systemic.

#### 2. **ASCII-Only Standard Violation (CRITICAL)**

The PR introduces non-ASCII Unicode characters into C# string literals. This is explicitly forbidden as it is known to cause cascading compiler failures.

*   **Standard:** `GEMINI.md` -> **CRITICAL: ASCII-Only in All C# String Literals**: "NEVER use emoji, curly quotes, em-dashes, Unicode arrows, or box-drawing in `Print()` or any string literal."
*   **Violation:**
    *   **File:** `V12_002.REAPER.cs`
    *   **Line:** 620: `"\u2713 Repair order submitted..."` (Check mark character)
    *   **Line:** 638: `"\u2717 ExecutingAccount is null..."` (Cross mark character)
    *   **Line:** 674: `"\u2717 CreateOrder returned null..."` (Cross mark character)
    *   **Line:** 696: `"\u2717 FAILED for {0}: {1}"` (Cross mark character)
*   **Remediation:** These Unicode characters must be replaced with their ASCII-compliant equivalents (e.g., `"[OK]"`, `"[FAIL]"`).

#### 3. **Scope Control Violation (High)**

The PR is a massive refactoring, not a surgical edit. It partitions a single file into over a dozen new partial classes.

*   **Standard:** `GEMINI.md` -> **Protocol Hardening (V12.Phase7) -> 1. Scope Control -> Surgical Edits**: "AI agents MUST restrict code modifications to the specific files requested in the Mission Brief. NEVER refactor unrelated files without explicit Director authorization."
*   **Violation:** This change fundamentally restructures the entire project codebase, which is far outside the scope of a surgical edit. This level of refactoring requires explicit Director authorization and a new mission brief.

### **Conclusion**

This pull request is **rejected**.

The violations of the core **Concurrency** and **ASCII-Only** standards are critical and introduce unacceptable risk. The violation of **Scope Control** demonstrates a failure to adhere to mission parameters.

**Required Actions:**
1.  Revert the concurrency model to the `lock(stateLock)` standard. Any proposal to change this standard must be made by amending `GEMINI.md` and gaining Director approval *before* implementation.
2.  Immediately remove all non-ASCII characters from string literals and replace them with their approved ASCII-safe substitutes.
3.  Deconstruct this monolithic PR into a series of smaller, surgical PRs if any of the isolated changes are still desired. The file system refactoring requires its own separate mission brief and approval.

This audit is complete.

Greptile Summary

This PR replaces the `stateLock`/`ipcLock` monitor pattern with a lock-free inline actor (`Enqueue` / `DrainActor` / `_drainToken`) and extends serialization coverage to `OnPositionUpdate`, UI entry hotkeys, `OnBreakevenButtonClick`, and `LogicAudit` test probes. The architecture is sound ÔÇö the non-recursive drain via `TriggerCustomEvent` correctly avoids stack growth from re-entrant broker callbacks, and primitive captures before `Enqueue` in `OnOrderUpdate`/`OnExecutionUpdate` guard against NT8 object recycling. **Key findings:** - **Critical ÔÇö missing `return` in FSM PendingCancel block** (`src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, lines 634ÔÇô680): The `return; // FSM-controlled cancel` that previously short-circuited the function was dropped during the lock-removal refactor. Execution now falls through to the A2-3 direction-aware delta rollback (`DeltaExpectedPositionLocked`) and `_dispatchSyncPendingExpKeys.Remove()` even when a follower replacement is already scheduled via `TriggerCustomEvent`. This can produce false-positive Reaper desync events and corrupt SIMA fleet synchronization while a replacement order is in flight. - **Logic ÔÇö stale `isRMAButtonClicked` read after `Enqueue` deferral** (`src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`, lines 153ÔÇô162): The UI-deactivation block that was previously gated behind a synchronous `ExecuteRMAEntryV2` call now reads `isRMAButtonClicked` before the entry executes. This causes premature panel deactivation on every click (even rejected trades) and can double-fire `SET_RMA_MODE|OFF` + the full CONFIG broadcast from `DeactivateRMAMode()` on successful entries. - **Style ÔÇö indentation mismatch in FSM block** (`src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, lines 611ÔÇô634): Dedented variable declarations inside the PendingCancel `if` block make brace pairing visually ambiguous and contributed to the missing `return` going unnoticed. - **Style ÔÇö stale version constant** (`V12_002.Constants.cs`): `Version = "Build 936"` contradicts `BUILD_TAG = "963"` in `src/V12_002.cs`.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge ÔÇö the missing `return` in the FSM PendingCancel block will cause incorrect delta rollbacks and premature SIMA dispatch-barrier releases on every follower entry replacement, risking ghost-position false positives under live multi-account trading. - The actor architecture and non-recursive drain are correctly implemented, and the majority of lock-removal changes are safe. However, the dropped `return;` in `src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` is a concrete regression: it executes the A2-3 `DeltaExpectedPositionLocked` rollback on FSM-controlled cancels, which the PR's own ghost-state immunity goals explicitly aim to prevent. The UI stale-read issue in `src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs` is a secondary behavior change. Both issues are small, targeted fixes. - `src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` requires the `return;` restoration before merge. `src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs` needs the `isRMAButtonClicked` block moved inside the `Enqueue` closure.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/V12_002.cs | Core file: removes `stateLock`/`ipcLock`, introduces the inline actor (`Enqueue`/`DrainActor`/`_drainToken`). The non-recursive drain via `TriggerCustomEvent` is correctly implemented. No issues found in the actor itself. | | src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs | Critical bug: the `return; // FSM-controlled cancel` statement was dropped from the PendingCancel FSM block. Execution now falls through to the A2-3 delta rollback and `_dispatchSyncPendingExpKeys.Remove`, which fire incorrectly for follower entry replacements, corrupting `expectedPositions` and releasing the SIMA dispatch barrier prematurely. Also has minor indentation inconsistency in the refactored FSM block. | | src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs | Entry hotkeys (L/S keys, MOMO, RMA chart click) are correctly wrapped in `Enqueue`. However, the `if (isRMAButtonClicked)` UI-deactivation block reads stale pre-entry state now that `ExecuteRMAEntryV2` is deferred, causing premature panel deactivation and potential double-broadcast on success. | | src/V12_002.LogicAudit.cs | Test-probe writes to `expectedPositions` correctly wrapped in `Enqueue` closure for actor-thread compliance. No issues found. | | src/V12_002.Trailing.cs | `OnBreakevenButtonClick` mutations correctly wrapped in per-position `Enqueue` closures with safe `capturedAnyArmed` capture. Lock-free stop order mutations look clean. No issues found. | | V12_002.Constants.cs | Placeholder stub file with a stale `Version = "Build 936"` constant that contradicts the `BUILD_TAG = "963"` in `src/V12_002.cs`. Minor cosmetic issue. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant UI as UI Thread participant CB as NT8 Callback Thread participant Q as ConcurrentQueue participant DA as DrainActor participant ST as Strategy State Note over UI,ST: Normal enqueue path (hotkey or chart click) UI->>Q: Enqueue(ExecuteLong / ExecuteMOMOEntry) UI->>DA: TryDrain acquires drainToken DA->>ST: cmd.Execute runs lock-free ST-->>DA: broker call re-triggers Enqueue internally Note over DA: Re-entrant Enqueue token already held, item queued DA->>Q: while TryDequeue picks up re-entrant item DA->>ST: re-entrant item processed DA->>DA: finally release token, check IsEmpty alt Queue not empty DA->>DA: TriggerCustomEvent schedules next DrainActor end Note over CB,ST: NT8 broker callback path CB->>Q: Enqueue(ProcessOnOrderUpdate) CB->>DA: TryDrain attempt alt Token free DA->>ST: ProcessOnOrderUpdate runs lock-free else Token held Note over CB: Item picked up by in-progress drain or next TriggerCustomEvent end Note over CB,ST: Bug - FSM PendingCancel path missing return CB->>Q: Enqueue follower cancel DA->>ST: PendingCancel block executes DA->>ST: TriggerCustomEvent schedules SubmitFollowerReplacement DA->>ST: Falls through to A2-3 delta rollback DA->>ST: DeltaExpectedPositionLocked fires incorrectly DA->>ST: dispatchSyncPendingExpKeys Remove fires prematurely ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`, line 153-162 ([link](https://github.com/mkalhitti-cloud/universal-or-strategy/blob/6f7d50e9cc04c7fb3d3950f3f374ede26fef4a4f/src/V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs#L153-L162)) **Stale `isRMAButtonClicked` read after `Enqueue` deferral ÔÇö UI deactivates RMA mode before entry executes** In the old synchronous code, `ExecuteRMAEntryV2` ran inline and called `DeactivateRMAMode()` ÔåÆ `isRMAButtonClicked = false` before the `if (isRMAButtonClicked)` guard. So the guard would be `false` and this UI-deactivation block would **not** run (deactivation was already performed with a full CONFIG broadcast inside `DeactivateRMAMode()`). With `Enqueue(ctx => ctx.ExecuteRMAEntryV2(...))`, the entry is now deferred. `isRMAButtonClicked` is still `true` when the guard is evaluated, so this block fires immediately on every chart click while RMA is armed ÔÇö even if the entry is subsequently rejected by the compliance gate, ATR guard, or position size check. Two consequences: 1. `SET_RMA_MODE|OFF` is sent to the panel before the entry result is known, causing a visual flicker (button disarms even on rejected trades). 2. If `ExecuteRMAEntryV2` succeeds, **both** this lightweight `SET_RMA_MODE|OFF` path **and** `DeactivateRMAMode()`'s full CONFIG broadcast will fire, producing two consecutive panel updates that can leave the UI in an inconsistent state. Consider moving this block inside the `Enqueue` closure, so it executes atomically with the entry decision, or gate it by checking the *result* of the entry rather than pre-entry state: ``` // Instead of reading isRMAButtonClicked on the UI thread, move deactivation logic inside the closure: bool wasRmaClicked = isRMAButtonClicked; Enqueue(ctx => { ctx.ExecuteRMAEntryV2(capturedRmaPrice, capturedDir, capturedRmaContracts); // isRMAButtonClicked is cleared by DeactivateRMAMode inside ExecuteRMAEntryV2; no extra deactivation needed here }); // Remove the if (isRMAButtonClicked) block below ```
Last reviewed commit: 6f7d50e

### ­ƒñû Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI)

Honorable Director,

I have completed my audit of the pull request as the Gemini CLI (Antigravity Auto-Auditor). My analysis was performed in strict accordance with the `GEMINI.md` project standards.

The pull request introduces a significant architectural refactoring, partitioning the strategy logic into multiple files and replacing the `lock(stateLock)` concurrency model with a queue-based actor model.

While the new model is sound in principle, its implementation is dangerously incomplete. Furthermore, a critical, build-breaking violation of the ASCII-only string literal standard has been identified.

**Recommendation: REJECT**

This pull request cannot be merged in its current state. It introduces severe race conditions and a known compiler-breaking issue.

---

### **Audit Findings**

#### **CRITICAL VIOLATION 1: Incomplete Concurrency Model Migration**

The PR removes the `lock(stateLock)` mechanism, which is a direct violation of the existing `GEMINI.md` standard. While it replaces it with a new actor model (`Enqueue`/`TryDrain`), this new model has not been applied to all code paths that mutate shared state. This leaves the system vulnerable to critical race conditions, data corruption, and unpredictable behavior.

The following entry points mutate shared state (e.g., `activePositions`, `entryOrders`, mode flags) without being serialized through the new `Enqueue` actor model:

1.  **File**: `V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`
    *   **Method**: `OnKeyDown(object sender, KeyEventArgs e)`
    *   **Context**: This UI event handler directly calls methods like `ExecuteLong()`, `ExecuteShort()`, `FlattenAll()`, and `ExecuteTargetAction()`. These methods or their callees directly mutate shared collections like `activePositions` and `entryOrders` without any concurrency protection.
    *   **Required Fix**: All logic within `OnKeyDown` that leads to state mutation must be wrapped in an `Enqueue()` call. Example: `Enqueue(ctx => ctx.ExecuteLong(orContracts));`

2.  **File**: `V12_002.UI.Callbacks.cs`
    *   **Method**: `OnChartClick(object sender, MouseButtonEventArgs e)`
    *   **Context**: This mouse event handler directly calls `ExecuteMOMOEntry()` and `ExecuteRMAEntryV2()`, which mutate shared state. It also directly mutates state flags like `isRMAModeActive`.
    *   **Required Fix**: The state-mutating logic must be dispatched to the actor queue via `Enqueue()`.

3.  **File**: `V12_002.cs`
    *   **Method**: `OnBarUpdate()`
    *   **Context**: This method, the core of the strategy's bar-by-bar logic, directly calls `ManageTrailingStops()`. The trailing stop logic reads and writes to `PositionInfo` objects within the `activePositions` collection, creating a race condition with order update callbacks that also modify this state.
    *   **Required Fix**: The call to `ManageTrailingStops()` and other state-mutating logic within `OnBarUpdate()` must be wrapped in `Enqueue()`.

#### **CRITICAL VIOLATION 2: Non-ASCII Characters in String Literals**

The code violates the `CRITICAL: ASCII-Only in All C# String Literals` standard. This is known to cause cascading compiler errors (Build 936 incident).

1.  **File**: `V12_002.REAPER.cs`
    *   **Lines**: `577`, `579`, `683`
    *   **Context**: The `Print()` statements in `ExecuteReaperRepair` use Unicode characters `\u2713` (Ô£ô) and `\u2717` (Ô£ù).
    *   **Violation**: Non-ASCII characters are strictly forbidden in string literals.
    *   **Required Fix**: Replace these Unicode characters with their approved ASCII equivalents, such as `[OK]` and `[FAIL]`.

---

### **Conclusion**

This PR fails the Zero-Regression Bot Audit. The incomplete concurrency refactoring introduces high-risk race conditions that were previously prevented by the `stateLock` standard. The ASCII violation will break the build.

The entire PR must be reworked to ensure that **all** code paths that mutate shared state are serialized through the new actor model. The ASCII violations must be expunged. Until these critical issues are resolved, merging this code would severely compromise system stability and reliability.

### ­ƒñû Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI)

Greetings. I am the Gemini CLI, the Antigravity Auto-Auditor. I have completed my forensic analysis of the pull request. The proposed changes contain **multiple critical violations** of the `GEMINI.md` project standards. This PR cannot be merged in its current state.

My findings are detailed below.

### **CRITICAL VIOLATION: Unauthorized Concurrency Model Replacement**

The PR removes the `lock(stateLock)` mechanism and replaces it with a custom actor-based concurrency model using `ConcurrentQueue` and `TriggerCustomEvent`. This is a direct and systemic violation of a foundational project standard.

-   **Standard (GEMINI.md):** "Concurrency: All state mutations (activePositions, expectedPositions) MUST be guarded by `lock(stateLock)`."
-   **Violation Analysis:** The `stateLock` object has been removed from `V12_002.cs`. Consequently, dozens of state mutations across multiple files (`V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`, `V12_002.Trailing.cs`, `V12_002.UI.IPC.cs`, etc.) are no longer guarded by the mandated `lock`. While the new actor model may be a valid concurrency pattern, it is not the approved standard. Introducing a secondary, complex concurrency model fragments our safety protocols and increases architectural debt. This change is an unauthorized overhaul of a core safety system.

    -   **File:** `V12_002.cs`
    -   **Context:** The declaration `private readonly object stateLock = new object();` has been deleted and replaced with the `Enqueue`/`TryDrain` actor model implementation. All subsequent `lock(stateLock)` blocks have been removed project-wide.

### **CRITICAL VIOLATION: Prohibited Cancel+Submit Pattern**

The implementation of `PropagateMasterTargetMove` in `V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` directly violates the FSM requirement for follower order replacements.

-   **Standard (GEMINI.md, 5. MOVE-SYNC):** "Any follower order cancel+resubmit MUST use the two-phase Replace FSM (`_followerReplaceSpecs` dict)." and "Never cancel+submit directly: Raw `Cancel()` followed immediately by `Submit()` is BANNED for follower orders -- it creates ghost orders."
-   **Violation Analysis:** The `PropagateMasterTargetMove` method performs a raw `pos.ExecutingAccount.Cancel(...)` followed by `pos.ExecutingAccount.Submit(...)` for a follower's target order. This bypasses the mandatory two-phase FSM, creating the exact race condition the standard is designed to prevent.

    -   **File:** `V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`
    -   **Context:** `PropagateMasterTargetMove` function. This code does not use the `_followerTargetReplaceSpecs` FSM as required.

### **VIOLATION: Non-ASCII Characters in String Literals**

The code introduces non-ASCII Unicode characters into `Print()` statements, which is strictly forbidden and known to break the NinjaTrader compiler.

-   **Standard (GEMINI.md, CRITICAL):** "NEVER use emoji, curly quotes, em-dashes, Unicode arrows, or box-drawing in `Print()` or any string literal."
-   **Violation Analysis:** Unicode characters are used for logging, which risks a cascading build failure.

    -   **File:** `V12_002.REAPER.cs`
    -   **Context:** Multiple `Print` statements use Unicode characters `\u2717` (Ô£ù) and `\u2713` (Ô£ô), for example: `Print($"[REAPER REPAIR] \u2717 No PositionInfo found for {0} ...")`. Allowed substitutions like `(X)` or `(OK)` must be used.

### **VIOLATION: Non-Surgical Edit**

This pull request is a massive, system-wide refactoring that partitions the primary strategy file into over 20 new files. This is not a "surgical edit."

-   **Standard (GEMINI.md, 1. Scope Control):** "AI agents MUST restrict code modifications to the specific files requested in the Mission Brief. NEVER refactor unrelated files without explicit Director authorization."
-   **Violation Analysis:** This change constitutes a major architectural overhaul, not a targeted fix. Such a change requires a dedicated mission brief and explicit authorization from the Director, as it fundamentally alters the structure and maintenance profile of the entire project.

### **Conclusion**

This pull request introduces multiple high-severity violations against established safety and architectural standards. The unauthorized replacement of the core concurrency model is particularly egregious and demonstrates a disregard for protocol stability.

**Directive:** **REJECT MERGE.** The branch must be reverted. A new mission brief must be issued by the Director to authorize any changes to the concurrency model or project structure. All identified violations must be addressed before any code can be reconsidered for integration.

## OMNI-AUDIT [Antigravity]: Phase 4  Ghost-State Gauntlet

**Builds audited post-remediation:** 963, 964, 965
**Branch:** `v12-inline-actor-refactor`

---

##  VERDICT: FAIL

The 963/964/965 remediation was **partially applied**. Two categories of violations survive:

---

###  Category A: `src/V12_002.Trailing.cs`  Naked Writes (HIGHEST SEVERITY)

These are in the **canonical `src/` files** that get compiled for production.

| Line | Code | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **L635** | `stopOrders[entryName] = newStop;` | `lock(stateLock){...}` stripped, **NO Enqueue added** |
| **L647** | `if (newStop != null) stopOrders[entryName] = newStop;` | Same  naked |

The diff shows these were precisely `lock (stateLock) { stopOrders[entryName] = newStop; }`  `stopOrders[entryName] = newStop;` with no enclosure replacement.

**Fix pattern:**
```csharp
var _capturedEntry = entryName;
var _capturedStop  = newStop;
if (_capturedStop != null)
    Enqueue(ctx => { ctx.stopOrders[_capturedEntry] = _capturedStop; });
```

---

###  Category B: Root-Level NEW Files  All Entry Modules (40+ violations)

The PR adds 24 new files at the **repo root** (no `src/` prefix). These have zero `Enqueue` patterns. Files with naked writes:

- `V12_002.Entries.FFMA.cs`  L191-192, L329-330, L474-475
- `V12_002.Entries.MOMO.cs`  L162-163
- `V12_002.Entries.OR.cs`  L227-228
- `V12_002.Entries.RMA.cs`  L111, L141, L339-340, L465-466
- `V12_002.Entries.Retest.cs`  L174, L188, L193, L316, L330, L334
- `V12_002.Entries.Trend.cs`  L230, L257, L424-425
- `V12_002.Orders.Management.cs`  L104, L412, L567, L851, L939-940, L1182...
- `V12_002.SIMA.cs`  L85, L103, L125, L475-477, L520-521, L563-565, L655-657
- `V12_002.REAPER.cs`  L685
- `V12_002.Symmetry.cs`  L487
- `V12_002.Trailing.cs`  L635, L647 (root copy)
- `V12_002.LogicAudit.cs`  L291, L295

All bear the stale comment: `// A1-1/A2-1: Null-abort rollback + stateLock wrap (Build 960 audit fix)`  misleading because stateLock was removed but no Enqueue was added.

---

###  CLEARED: `src/V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs` (False Positive)

Automated scan flagged L423, L569, L730, L1084-1088. These are **NOT violations.**
`ProcessOnOrderUpdate` is a private method delegated via:
```csharp
Enqueue(ctx => ctx.ProcessOnOrderUpdate(_o, _lp, _sp, _q, _f, _af, _os, _t, _ne)); // L172
```
All collection mutations inside it run exclusively within the drain.  Architecture is sound.

---

###  Recursion & Deadlock Safety: PASS

- CAS guard on `_drainToken` intact  TryDrain remains recursion-safe
- `TriggerCustomEvent` used correctly for deferred work
- Zero `lock(stateLock)` blocks in added lines
- Zero broker API calls holding locks inside actor

###  ASCII Compliance: PASS

Full scan of all `+` lines for characters outside `[\x00-\x7F]`  **zero matches.**

---

## Action Required  Build 966

**Minimum patch required:**
1. `src/V12_002.Trailing.cs` L635 and L647  wrap `stopOrders[entryName] = newStop` in `Enqueue`
2. Clarify/fix root-level NEW files  either delete them (if `src/` is authoritative) or add `Enqueue` wrappers to all state writes

**DO NOT MERGE.**

### ­ƒñû Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini 2.5 Pro via Vertex AI)

Greetings. I am the Gemini CLI, the Antigravity Auto-Auditor. I have completed my analysis of this pull request against the `GEMINI.md` project standards.

The pull request introduces a significant architectural refactoring, partitioning the monolithic strategy file into multiple, feature-specific partial classes. While this modularity is a positive step for code organization, the implementation contains critical violations of our core engineering and safety standards.

This pull request **FAILS** the Zero-Regression Bot Audit.

### Systematic Review of Violations

#### 1. CRITICAL VIOLATION: Concurrency Model Departure

The `GEMINI.md` standard is unequivocal: **"All state mutations (activePositions, expectedPositions) MUST be guarded by `lock(stateLock)`."**

This pull request systematically removes all `lock(stateLock)` guards across the entire codebase and replaces them with an `Enqueue` method, implementing a new actor-based concurrency model. While this pattern can be valid, it is a direct and fundamental violation of the current, mandated project standard. This change affects dozens of locations across all new `V12_002.*.cs` files where state is mutated.

**Example Violation (`V12_002.Entries.FFMA.cs`, L193):**
```diff
-                lock (stateLock)
-                {
-                    activePositions[entryName] = pos;
-                    entryOrders[entryName] = entryOrder;
-                }
+                { var _en966ap = entryName; var _p966ap = pos; Enqueue(ctx => { ctx.activePositions[_en966ap] = _p966ap; }); }
+                { var _en966 = entryName; var _eo966 = entryOrder; Enqueue(ctx => { ctx.entryOrders[_en966] = _eo966; }); }
```
This pattern is repeated for all mutations of `activePositions`, `entryOrders`, and `expectedPositions`, representing a wholesale replacement of the mandated concurrency protocol.

#### 2. CRITICAL VIOLATION: Non-ASCII Characters in String Literals

The `GEMINI.md` standard explicitly states: **"NEVER use emoji, curly quotes, em-dashes, Unicode arrows, or box-drawing in `Print()` or any string literal."** This is to prevent catastrophic NinjaTrader compiler failures (Build 936 incident).

The file `V12_002.REAPER.cs` contains multiple `Print` statements with Unicode characters, which will break the build.

**Violations:**
*   **File:** `V12_002.REAPER.cs`
    *   `Print($"[REAPER REPAIR] \u2717 ExecutingAccount is null for {accountName}");` (L598)
    *   `Print($"[REAPER REPAIR] \u2717 CreateOrder returned null for {accountName}");` (L661)
    *   `Print($"[REAPER REPAIR] \u2713 Repair order submitted for {accountName} under key={repairEntryName}: " + ...);` (L685)
    *   `Print($"[REAPER REPAIR] \u2717 FAILED for {accountName}: {ex.Message}");` (L702)

The characters `\u2717` (BALLOT X) and `\u2713` (CHECK MARK) are not ASCII and are expressly forbidden.

#### 3. VIOLATION: Concurrency Data Race

The removal of `lock(stateLock)` has introduced severe data race bugs. The new actor model (`Enqueue`) only protects state that is explicitly mutated within its closures. Several shared collections are now accessed by multiple threads without any protection.

**Violations:**
*   **File:** `V12_002.REAPER.cs`
    *   The `HashSet<string>` collections `_repairInFlight` and `_reaperNakedStopInFlight` are not thread-safe. They are written to by the `reaperThread` and read/written by the main strategy thread via `TriggerCustomEvent`. This is an unprotected concurrent access pattern that can lead to collection corruption or incorrect state evaluation. The `lock(stateLock)` that previously protected these has been removed.
*   **File:** `V12_002.SIMA.cs`
    *   The `HashSet<string>` collection `_dispatchSyncPendingExpKeys` is written by the strategy thread but read by the `reaperThread` in `AuditSingleFleetAccount` without a lock. This read operation (`.Contains()`) is not thread-safe and can race with writes.

#### 4. VIOLATION: Inconsistent FSM Replace Pattern

The `GEMINI.md` standard for the MOVE-SYNC pattern is strict: **"Any follower order cancel+resubmit MUST use the two-phase Replace FSM"** and **"Never cancel+submit directly"**.

The codebase is inconsistent in its adherence to this rule.

**Violation:**
*   **File:** `V12_002.Orders.Callbacks.cs`
    *   The method `PropagateMasterTargetMove` performs a direct `Cancel()` followed by `CreateOrder()` and `Submit()` for follower target orders. This is a banned pattern that creates ghost orders.

**Contradiction:**
*   The method `MoveSpecificTarget` in `V12_002.Trailing.cs` correctly implements a two-phase FSM using `_followerTargetReplaceSpecs` for the same operation. The codebase must be consistent.

### Conclusion

This pull request cannot be merged. The violations are numerous and severe, ranging from a fundamental departure from the mandated concurrency model to critical build-breaking errors and data race conditions.

**Action Required:**
1.  **Revert the concurrency model** to strictly use `lock(stateLock)` for all shared state mutations as per `GEMINI.md`.
2.  **Remove all non-ASCII characters** from string literals in `V12_002.REAPER.cs`.
3.  **Fix all data races** by properly guarding access to non-thread-safe collections (`HashSet`) that are accessed across threads.
4.  **Refactor `PropagateMasterTargetMove`** to use the two-phase FSM, making it consistent with `MoveSpecificTarget` and compliant with the MOVE-SYNC protocol.

This submission is rejected. A full rewrite of the violating sections is required to meet project standards.
